title: Lesson 398
transcript: Password vaults, you may well have used password vaults. They're often built into web browsers nowadays. A lot of us have them installed on our home computers, things like Dashlane, LastPass. They're used in corporate environments all the time. So what are password vaults? They are application that's used for storing passwords. They're often referred to as password managers, by the way. So here's the whole idea behind this, right? We as humans, or most of us are not very good at remembering a whole lot of passwords, let alone a whole lot of really complex, good passwords. If you force someone to choose a different, like, you know, have passwords for 20 different systems, pretty much guaranteed they're gonna use the same password on every single one of those 20 systems or just change like a number or something. And that's obviously a big security weakness, right? Because if one system is compromised and the attacker manages to figure out the user's credentials on one system, it's highly likely that the user has used the same credentials, right? The same email address and password on a bunch of other systems. So the whole fundamental idea behind password vaults is this is a tool that's meant to store passwords, to remember passwords for us. And that way, the whole reason you would want to use one of these things is that you can then store a unique, really long 20, 30 character complex password for every single different system. And it's the password manager that's going to remember those passwords and not you, the user. So that's the huge idea behind password vaults. And they're very beneficial and they can certainly help from a security perspective to make it so that you don't have to remember a whole bunch of complex passwords and you are able to use a different password on every system. they can also have some benefits from in a corporate perspective if you're in the difficult situation of having like older legacy systems that you can't have like say unique admin accounts on so there's like one shared admin account You can use password vaults to sort of like check out the admin password or share it across multiple employees. Not great, but it's workable. But like with any security tool, right? There's also major downsides. and one of the major downsides of a password vault is it's a massive single point of failure if your password vault is compromised now of course the attacker has access to everything so you want to be really careful about using these things and the master password the master password is the password that you use to unlock your password vault and get access to all your passwords contained within You really want to try and make sure that's a very secure password. And best practice would also be use some sort of multi-factor authentication to unlock the password vault. But yeah, that's the idea. Password vaults.
